catalyst.event: Q2 2026 interim results disclosure — first full half reflecting nationwide Class C OTC online sales reopening and outpatient coordination (门诊统筹) settlement normalization; market will read same-store sales growth (SSSG) and gross margin trajectory as the binary signal on whether the pharmacy retail business model has structurally repaired
catalyst.date_or_window: 2026-08-25 to 2026-08-31 (Da Shenlin historically discloses interims in late August; FY2025 annual was filed April 2026)
catalyst.source: SSE disclosure calendar; company FY2025 annual report filing pattern

rightIf_conditions: H1 2026 interim (Aug 2026) SSSG ≥ +2% YoY AND gross margin ≥ 34.5%
FY2026 NI guidance/consensus revises upward to ≥1.4B (from current implied ~1.3B)
Forward P/E re-rates above 15x within 6 months
wrongIf_conditions: Operating margin compresses below 9.0% in any reported quarter through 2026
Store-count growth slows below 5% YoY (signals expansion runway exhausted, per watchlist seed)
what_changes_our_mind: H1 2026 interim (2026-08-25 window) shows SSSG still ≤0% AND gross margin <33.5% — this confirms the FY2025 NI lift was one-off (e.g., inventory writeback, subsidy timing) rather than sustainable margin recapture, in which case the consolidation thesis is invalid and we exit
variant_view_one_sentence: Market believes Da Shenlin's FY2025 NI +35% is low-base noise → We believe it's margin-driven proof of consolidation pricing power returning → Mechanism is H1 2026 interim printing positive SSSG forces re-rating from 13x to 17x fwd P/E.

expected_pnl_asymmetry.downside_if_wrong: -12% to -15% (downside floor near 52W low 16.78, ~10% below spot, plus risk of further de-rating to 11x fwd ≈ 15.8)
